title: Using Panic selector (0x4e487b71) instead of custom error selector
explanation: 0x4e487b71 is keccak256('Panic(uint256)') — Solidity's reserved selector for compiler-generated panics like division by zero, array index out of bounds, and arithmetic overflow. Using it manually confuses debuggers, block explorers, and client-side error decoders which expect a uint256 parameter after the selector. Custom errors should use their own keccak256-derived 4-byte selectors.
why_wrong: Block explorers and SDKs treat 0x4e487b71 as a Panic and try to decode a uint256 parameter that isn't there. Error monitoring tools misclassify the revert. Auditors flag it as a code smell. It also conflicts if Solidity ever generates a real Panic in the same function.
gas_impact: Zero gas difference — this is a correctness and tooling bug not a gas bug
